data.title: Russian Power Engineers Develop New Solutions to Hunt Illegal Crypto Miners
data.description:

source: https://cryptonews.com/news/russian-power-engineers-develop-new-solutions-to-hunt-illegal-crypto-miners/

Pump. fun is no longer untouchable, and it now risks fading into obscurity as it has to […]

data.title: XRP Frenzy Builds: Over $1 Billion in Open Interest Signals Breakout Tension
data.description:
XRP’s price has climbed to $2. 61 in the last 24 hours, and has been able to keep a steady pace, registering a 22% gain over the past seven days. Traders are piling into futures contracts. A notable performance has been in the Open interest department, which jumped by more than 40%
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/altcoin/xrp-frenzy-builds-over-1-billion-in-open-interest-signals-breakout-tension/

data.title: Ethereum Foundation aims to secure the future with trillion-dollar security initiative
data.description:
The Ethereum Foundation (EF) has unveiled the Trillion-Dollar Security (1TS) initiative, a long-term plan to raise Ethereum’s security standards in line with its growing importance in global finance. The Foundation said it aims to position Ethereum as a network capable of securely holding trillions of dollars in assets for individuals and institutions.
source: https://cryptoslate.com/ethereum-foundation-aims-to-secure-the-future-with-trillion-dollar-security-initiative/

data.title: Dogecoin active addresses surge by 528% — Will DOGE price follow?
data.description:
Key Takeaways: Dogecoin's active addresses surged 528% to 469,477. DOGE’s futures open interest rose 70% to $1. 65 billion, indicating strong speculative interest. On May 13, Dogecoin (DOGE) witnessed a staggering 528% increase in active addresses, soaring from 74,640 to 469,477, signaling robust network activity and growing investor interest
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/dogecoin-active-addresses-surge-by-528-will-doge-price-follow?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Polymarket's new market creation hits record high in April, number of active traders drops
data.description: According to data from The Block, Polymarket's new market creation reached an all-time high in April, with more than 7,000 prediction markets launched, up 20.7% from 5,800 in March. Despite the surge in market creation, the number of active traders has dropped from 462,000 in January to 330,000 in April, and trading volume has stabilized, with total bets of less than $900 million in April, down from $2.6 billion in January. The report said this may indicate that the user base is shifting toward more centralized market creators.

data.title: Director of the U.S. Digital Assets Committee: It is unfair to question the Trump family's involvement in the crypto industry
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: At the Consensus 2025 conference held in Toronto, Robert Hines (Bo Hines), executive director of the US Presidential Digital Asset Advisory Committee, said that the questioning of the Trump family's involvement in the cryptocurrency industry was "unfair" and criticized the Biden administration's crackdown on the crypto industry as an "unprecedented war." He pointed out that the current government will be committed to clearing "Operation Chokepoint 2.0" regulatory barriers, promoting the free interaction between traditional finance and crypto assets, and emphasized that the White House's establishment of a full-time crypto affairs commissioner is an important policy turning point.

data.title: U.S. Government Aims to Ease Crypto Regulations, Says Official
data.description: According to Odaily, at the Consensus 2025 conference in Toronto, Robert Hines, Executive Director of the U.S. Presidential Digital Asset Advisory Committee, defended the Trump family's involvement in the cryptocurrency industry, calling the criticism "unfair." He criticized the regulatory actions during the Biden administration as an "unprecedented war" on the crypto sector. Hines emphasized that the current administration is committed to removing regulatory barriers similar to "Operation Chokepoint 2.0" to facilitate the interaction between traditional finance and crypto assets. He also highlighted the establishment of a dedicated crypto affairs commissioner at the White House as a significant policy shift.

data.title: Cantor Fitzgerald Chairman Addresses Tether Reserve Rumors at Consensus 2025
data.description: According to Odaily, during the Consensus 2025 conference held in Toronto, Cantor Fitzgerald Chairman Brandon Lutnick addressed rumors regarding Tether's reserves. Lutnick stated, "I personally checked many of their reserves, and we have proven many rumors to be false." He noted that during the initial collaboration with Tether, there were numerous rumors suggesting that Tether did not have adequate reserves. Lutnick emphasized that despite Bloomberg's reports claiming he interned for Tether in Lugano, Switzerland, he clarified, "That is not true."

data.title: Wintermute to establish US headquarters in New York, appoints Ron Hammond as policy director
data.description: Crypto market maker Wintermute announced that it will set up its US headquarters in downtown New York on June 1, its first office in the United States. "As the US policy becomes more friendly to digital assets and blockchain innovation, we decided to move quickly and take root in New York City, the world's financial capital," said Evgeny Gaevoy, CEO of Wintermute. In addition, Wintermute appointed policy veteran Ron Hammond as director of policy and advocacy. Hammond was previously the senior director of government relations at the Blockchain Association and was the head of financial services policy for Republican Congressman Warren Davidson. "As the US regulatory environment becomes more constructive, we see tremendous opportunities to promote responsible innovation and deepen engagement with policymakers and industry stakeholders," he said.

data.title: Analyst: Robinhood's $179 million acquisition of WonderFi could increase revenue by 10%
data.description: Robinhood has acquired Canadian crypto platform WonderFi Technologies, which owns two regulated trading platforms Bitbuy and Coinsquare, for an all-cash $250 million (about $179 million). Mizuho analyst Dan Dolev called the deal a "key first step into the Canadian market" and expected it to bring Robinhood "approximately $250 million in annual revenue," or "close to 10% growth."

data.title: Pantera Capital CEO Predicts Long-Term Bitcoin Growth at Consensus 2025
data.description:
According to Odaily, at the Consensus 2025 conference in Toronto, Pantera Capital's founder and CEO, Dan Morehead, expressed optimism about Bitcoin's potential for long-term returns. He emphasized Pantera's continued confidence in the asset class and advised investors to diversify into a broad range of tokens and venture equity to seize evolving market opportunities.

Morehead revealed that Pantera has achieved an 86% profit in its portfolio and has invested in 22 unicorn companies valued over $1 billion. He criticized the United States for its slow pace in cryptocurrency regulation, noting that 90% of crypto transactions and protocols occur outside the U.S., which he deemed problematic. However, he remained hopeful, suggesting that electoral victories could unlock significant opportunities, potentially leading to a return of capital and innovation to the U.S. in the coming years.
